title: To amend the Cybersecurity Enhancement Act of 2014 to make improvements to the Federal Cyber Scholarship for Service Program, and for other purposes.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
plain_summary: This bill changes the Federal Cyber Scholarship for Service Program in two ways. First, it lets students get scholarships for up to five years instead of just three years. Second, it removes limits on how much student loan debt can be forgiven for people in the program. The program gives scholarships to students studying cybersecurity and related fields like artificial intelligence, and students must work for the government after graduating.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.

sections.title: Section 1. Improvements to Federal Cyber Scholarship for Service Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Cybersecurity Enhancement Act of 2014. The changes update a program that gives scholarships to students who study cybersecurity in exchange for working for the government later. First, the bill extends the maximum time students can receive these scholarships from three years to five years. Second, it changes the rules about loan forgiveness for students in this program. The new rules say that the full amount of student loans can be forgiven, no matter what other limits normally apply to federal student loans under the Higher Education Act or related government rules and policies.
